file_name: ADMA BioManufacturing, LLC -  Amendment #3 to Manufacturing Agreement .txt

perturbation.original_text: Prices for such [***] Batches of Product shall be in accordance with Section 3.2 of the Agreement (as amended in Amendment #2). BPC Initials ___ Sanofi Pasteur Initials ___ 1 Confidential treatment has been requested with respect to portions of this agreement as indicated by "[***]" and such confidential portions have been deleted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to Rule 24b-2 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The Parties agree to amend the Agreement to impose on ADMA an obligation to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product for that period stalling from Q4 2018 up to Q4 2019, as further specified in Exhibit A attached hereto. Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product (the "Minimum Volume") of Product during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages, and not a penalty, amounting to $[***] ([***]) USD.
perturbation.changed_text: Prices for such [***] Batches of Product may be adjusted based on market conditions, notwithstanding Section 3.2 of the Agreement (as amended in Amendment #2). BPC Initials ___ Sanofi Pasteur Initials ___ 1 Confidential treatment has been requested with respect to portions of this agreement as indicated by "[***]" and such confidential portions have been deleted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to Rule 24b-2 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The Parties agree to amend the Agreement to impose on ADMA an obligation to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product for that period stalling from Q4 2018 up to Q4 2019, as further specified in Exhibit A attached hereto. Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product (the "Minimum Volume") of Product during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages, and not a penalty, amounting to $[***] ([***]) USD.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be' to 'may be adjusted', we introduce ambiguity regarding the pricing. While Section 3.2 outlines a fixed price, the changed text allows for price adjustments based on unspecified 'market conditions'. This contradicts the expectation of fixed pricing as per Section 3.2, leading to potential disputes. The contradiction occurs because the changed text provides an exception to the pre-defined pricing arrangement outlined in the agreement. The Updated Supply Plan in Exhibit A and the liquidated damages in case of not meeting the minimum supply are based on the prices, which will now be ambiguous due to potential 'market condition' adjustment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to amend the Agreement to impose on ADMA an obligation to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product for that period stalling from Q4 2018 up to Q4 2019, as further specified in Exhibit A attached hereto. Should ADMA fail to supply a minimum of [***] Batches of Product (the "Minimum Volume") of Product during the time period as specified in this Amendment #3, ADMA agrees that Sanofi Pasteur shall be entitled to obtain from ADMA as liquidated damages, and not a penalty, amounting to $[***] ([***]) USD. ADMA accepts and declares that the amount of the liquidated damages is a fair and equitable compensation, and not a penalty, for such failure in reaching the volume commitment within the timelines agreed herein and in regard to the value and use of the Source Plasma. In addition to the Minimum Volume of Product to be manufactured by ADMA, should ADMA deliver the Minimum Volume of Product but fail to meet the Updated Supply Plan as provided in Exhibit A as attached hereto and made an integral part hereof, then it is agreed upon by the Parties that ADMA shall pay to Sanofi Pasteur an amount equal to $[***] ([***]) USD for each Batch of Product that is less than the agreed upon quantity in Exhibit A, as liquidated damages, and not as a penalty.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that prices for batches of product 'shall be in accordance with Section 3.2'. The changed text modifies this to 'may be adjusted based on market conditions, notwithstanding Section 3.2'. This introduces a direct contradiction with Section 3.2 because the original phrasing mandates adherence to a specific pricing structure, while the changed text allows for deviation based on unspecified market conditions, thus making the pricing under Section 3.2 uncertain. This directly contradicts the contradicted text's reliance on specific prices for calculating liquidated damages as outlined in Exhibit A, making it unclear how damages are calculated if prices fluctuate due to 'market conditions' and potentially rendering the damage calculation unenforceable. This falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it introduces ambiguity in a key obligation (pricing) that directly conflicts with other sections relying on the original, fixed price arrangement.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall use best efforts to complete such amendment to the Quality Agreement within 60 days after the Amendment Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties may explore the possibility of amending the Quality Agreement at their convenience; no specific timeline is mandated.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes a 'best efforts' obligation within a defined timeframe (60 days). The changed text replaces this with a discretionary 'may explore' approach without a mandated timeline. This introduces a contradiction because the original Agreement terms would assume a prompt and diligent effort to amend the Quality Agreement, while the changed text allows for indefinite delay. The other terms from the agreement which rely on Quality Agreement, now are subjected to this changed behavior.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All other terms of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect except to the extent superseded by the terms of this Amendment #3. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereby have caused this Amendment #3 to the Agreement to be executed and the persons signing below warrant that they are duly authorized to sign for and on behalf of their respective Parties. Made in two original copies. Sanofi Pasteur, S.A. By:/s/ Vincent Hingot Name: Vincent Hingot Title: Senior Vice President Industrial Affairs Date: 22 Dec. 2017 ADMA BioManufacturing, LLC By: /s/ Adam Grossman Name: Adam Grossman Title: President & CEO Date: 12-21-2017 BPC Initials ___ Sanofi Pasteur Initials ___ 5 Confidential treatment has been requested with respect to portions of this agreement as indicated by "[***]" and such confidential portions have been deleted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to Rule 24b-2 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. Exhibit A [***]
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a 'best efforts' obligation to complete the Quality Agreement amendment within 60 days. The changed text replaces this with a discretionary 'may explore' option lacking a defined timeframe. This introduces a contradiction with the initial 'Agreement' terms that assumed prompt and diligent effort to amend the Quality Agreement, while the altered text permits indefinite delay. The 'Contradicted Text' states that 'All other terms of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect except to the extent superseded by the terms of this Amendment #3'. However, the change fundamentally alters the obligation regarding the Quality Agreement amendment, creating a contradiction regarding the expected timeline and effort, thus the terms from the agreement which rely on Quality Agreement, are now subjected to this changed behavior and the original agreement cannot be reasonably interpreted as compatible.
